gate0.source_grade_rationale: Reuters is a primary international newswire; first entry on the screener swarm's sources.signal_gate.allowed list; no aggregator intermediary.

entities.sector: Energy — LNG production and export (GICS: Energy / Oil, Gas & Consumable Fuels)
entities.geography: Gulf region (Middle East); specific country not stated in headline

materiality_math: Base 60 (material relevance + high-severity force majeure, pulled back from 70 for sector_only unnamed issuer) + novelty 21 (0.85 × 25, rounded) − penalties 0 (new_event: no duplicate/same-event deduction) + overrides 0 (no official filing, enforcement, or default) = 81/100
routing_reason: Materiality 81 clears the 70-point PROMOTE threshold. A confirmed force majeure on June-July LNG loadings from a major Gulf producer is a high-novelty supply shock that can re-price spot LNG, alter buyer procurement, and move equity valuations across LNG shipping, regasification, and diversified energy. The sector_only linkage does not suppress routing — it shapes Phase 1 beneficiary work.

sources.claim_supported: Force majeure declared by a major Gulf LNG producer on June-July cargo loadings due to compressor train failure
